frame: active_goal failure mode: contamination after pivot. User starts project-architecture conversation, then explicitly pivots to a different, unrelated topic. Several turns of B-work happen. Then user asks an ambiguous question about B.

turns.user: I'm designing a notification service. Should it be a separate microservice or part of the monolith?
turns.expected: active_goal='decide notification service architecture: microservice vs monolith'; modal_branches += two options

turns.user: What's the throughput estimate where the microservice case wins?
Actually, set this aside. I need to figure out something else first — I'm getting flaky CI runs and want to debug those before anything else.
turns.expected: CLEAN PIVOT — active_goal transitions to 'debug flaky CI runs'; current_frame updates; previous goal preserved as dormant or retired

turns.user: The failures are in the integration test suite, intermittent, no pattern I can see.
How do I get more data on which test is flaking?
turns.expected: model suggests instrumentation (rerun stats, timing histograms, etc)

turns.user: (after instrumentation, two tests identified as flaking — both touch the redis client.)
turns.expected: referents += [tool_result_N about redis client involvement]; current_frame may shift to redis-specific

PROBE — ambiguous; could refer to abandoned notification-service throughput (turn 2) OR redis throughput in CI context (since redis is now in current_frame). PASS: model resolves ambiguity in favor of active context (CI/redis), or asks; if defaulting, should be active-goal-aligned (B = redis throughput in CI), NOT retired-goal (A = notification service). FAIL: model defaults to retired-goal interpretation, drags notification-service context into pivoted conversation

pressure_test_targeted_criterion: no goal drift; clean pivot is honored
pressure_test_failure_mode: post-pivot goal drift — user pivots from goal A to goal B; Bearing-on contaminates goal-B responses with leftover goal-A context that should have been cleanly retired
